workload: 48 requests, 50% hot shared prefix / 50% unique, concurrency 8
interpretation: Strong hardware concentration on H100 is ALLOWED and occurs (40 vs 8) because its configured profile is genuinely ~5x faster — this is optimal, not a fairness violation.
Concentration is NOT 100%: MI350X still receives 8 requests when H100's live queue/congestion term grows — heterogeneous profiles respected, no herding.
NO artificial utilization-fairness reward: traffic is NOT split evenly by construction.
Both runtimes are REAL vLLM (H100 0.23 + MI350X ROCm 0.21) — NOT a real-vLLM-vs-mini-HF comparison.
